gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/327)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/327)
text: قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) مَنْ رَضِيَ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا بِمَا يُجْزِيهِ كَانَ أَيْسَرُ مَا فِيهَا يَكْفِيهِ وَ مَنْ لَمْ يَرْضَ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا بِمَا يُجْزِيهِ لَمْ يَكُنْ فِيهَا شَيْ‏ءٌ يَكْفِيهِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from a number of our companions, from Hanan Bin Sadeyr, raising it, said,

‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The one who is pleased from the world with whatever his apportionment is, the least of what is in it would suffice him; and the one who is not pleased from the world with what his apportionment is, there is nothing in it which would suffice him’.
en.sarwar: 11. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from a number of our people have narrated from Hannan ibn Sadir in a marfu’ manner from Amir al- Mu’minin (Ali ibn abu Talib) (a.s.) who has said the following: “Whoever is content, with worldly matters, with bare necessities, the minimum suffices him. Whoever is not content. with worldly matters, with bare necessities, then nothing will be able to suffice him.”
